_doc: P4-01 step 2 + A-U13 (Skill 6 v2, master-spec section A) -- persona-match regression corpus (40 labeled request -> expected-persona cases). Runs against the REAL, shipped 99-persona catalog (22-book-to-persona-coaching-leadership-system/persona-categories.json), never a synthetic fixture, so it measures actual production match quality. Each `request` is phrased the way a real board task/content request reads (drawing on request shapes named in the P4/spec cluster: content/comms tasks, sales scripts, coaching notes, marketing copy). `dimension` selects which pure matcher function is exercised: 'topic' -> persona_blend.match_topic_persona(catalog, request); 'audience' -> persona_blend.match_audience_persona(catalog, request) (request is then read as an audience LABEL, not a task). `intent_tag` (A-U13, additive) classifies WHY each case was drafted: 'topic-expertise' / 'audience-label' are the original P4-01 shapes (request reads as a task or an audience description, scored on topics[]/audiences[] tag overlap); 'emotional-register' cases (C21-C30) are phrased with an emotionally-toned ask (calming, reassuring, boundary-setting, grief/shame-aware, grit/resilience-building) so the corpus exercises the schema-1.4 (A-U3) emotional_register-carrying personas' topic/audience tags, not just their scalar emotional_register field (no dedicated emotional-register matcher function exists -- these cases still resolve via the same pure match_topic_persona/match_audience_persona tag-overlap functions, and each case's `note` records the winning persona's actual emotional_register value for traceability); 'conversion-style' cases (C31-C40) are phrased as closing/conversion-oriented asks (stack-closes, challenge-closes, negotiation, discovery-call scripting) exercising the schema-1.4 conversion_style-carrying personas the same way, with the winning persona's conversion_style value recorded in `note`. The gate (test-persona-match-regression-corpus.py) requires >=90% of cases to land on `expected_persona_id`. This corpus is a drift lock: an unrelated change to _tag_hit/_stem/ranking that quietly degrades match quality trips this gate before it reaches prod. A-U13 grew this corpus from 20 to 40 cases per master-spec v2 A-U13 (ONB, P2, dep A-U3): the original 20 (C01-C20) are UNCHANGED (byte-identical id/dimension/request/expected_persona_id/note); C21-C40 are net-new, each individually verified via a live run of persona_blend.match_topic_persona/match_audience_persona against the current shipped catalog before being locked into this file (never hand-guessed).
